response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but his profile does not match this role's mandatory requirements. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014–2015, far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++. He lists SQL and MySQL but no SQL Server or Oracle, and his financial exposure (RIDE Capital) is fintech product work in TypeScript, not fixed income or capital markets systems. The last decade of his career centers on web and full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, so the core skill and domain gaps are too large for a mid-senior capital-markets C++ consulting engagement.
strengths: Experienced polyglot engineer with low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus DevOps and messaging-adjacent infrastructure experience
Excellent communication skills, demonstrated through conference talks, multi-day workshops, and fluent English
concerns: Only about seven months of professional C++ experience (2014–2015), versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain experience; RIDE Capital was TypeScript-based fintech product work
No SQL Server or Oracle experience listed, and no Summit platform or Python exposure to offset the gaps

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ depth vs. 5–10 year mandatory requirement
No fixed income / capital markets domain expertise
Strong general engineering, performance, and communication skills
